response.justification: Candidate is an experienced full-stack software engineer with strong web, backend, and some embedded/DLang work, but the resume shows no SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, AI accelerator, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or deep-learning workload optimization experience. The role is a Principal Performance Architect for AI accelerator hardware, which requires a fundamentally different specialization than this candidate's web/product engineering background. Educational path is a BS in CS with ~13 years of experience (meets the 8+ years bar), but the experience is not relevant to the domain.
strengths: 13+ years of professional software engineering experience across multiple stacks
Some low-latency/high-throughput systems work (Dunnhumby/Sociomantic micro-services in DLang)
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling, open-source contributions
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, or chip architecture experience
No demonstrated AI/deep-learning workload, GPU, SIMD/MIMD, or accelerator performance analysis background
Career trajectory is web/product/full-stack engineering, not systems/performance architecture at the hardware level

key_factors.factor: Solid general software engineering tenure and breadth
